title: Intensely Smoky Irish Bamboo Shoot Stew
description: This earthy and silky stew features bamboo shoots in a light Irish dish with a smoky flavor, perfect for those looking for a sugar-free friendly meal.
instructions: In a large pot, cook the smoked bacon over medium heat until crispy.
Remove the bacon from the pot and set aside, leaving the grease behind.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the bamboo shoots, chicken broth, thyme, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the cooked bacon back into the pot and stir to combine.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
